item.title: Tillis Signals Openness to Warsh Vote If Congress Adds Fed Probe
item.description: Republican Senator Thom Tillis signaled openness to advancing Kevin Warsh’s nomination to be Federal Reserve chair if an ongoing US criminal inquiry into the central bank is dropped and Congress launches its own investigation.

item.title: OpenAI Unveils New Image Model That’s Better at Charts and Diagrams
item.description: OpenAI is releasing an update to its artificial intelligence image-generating software that it says will let users create accurate, complex charts and scientific diagrams, part of a bid by the company to make its technology more appealing to professionals.

item.title: Paraguay Leaves Key Rate at 5.5% on Stable Inflation Outlook
item.description: Paraguay’s central bank held its benchmark interest rate unchanged for a second-consecutive month at 5.5% amid a stable inflation outlook.

item.title: Fed’s Waller Calls for Shakeup of Central Bank’s Operations
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller called for a shakeup in the US central bank’s operations, arguing that key functions should be centralized and subject to less consensus-building among its 12 regional reserve banks.

item.title: Here comes the food price inflation
item.description: Input costs are already on the rise

item.title: Kalshi to Expand Crypto Wagers With Perpetual Futures Push
item.description: Kalshi Inc. is getting closer to launching a new product that will take it deeper into crypto trading with so-called perpetual futures.

item.title: Deutsche Telekom Weighs Full Combination With T-Mobile
item.description: Deutsche Telekom AG is considering a full combination with its American arm T-Mobile US Inc., a move that would create a multinational telecom group and rank as the largest-ever public M&A deal, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: Apple’s Cook Says He’s ‘Healthy,’ Will Be Chairman for Long Time
item.description: Apple Inc.’s Tim Cook, who’s stepping down as chief executive officer later this year, told employees on Tuesday that he is “healthy” and plans to serve as executive chairman for a long time.

item.title: Michael Dell Gives $750 Million for Medical Hub at UT Austin
item.description: Michael Dell famously started his computer business from a University of Texas dorm room. Now he’s giving back to the school with one of the largest philanthropic commitments in American higher education.

item.title: Cook’s Show of Fealty to Trump Is an Important Part of His Apple Legacy
item.description: The CEO traded photo ops for concessions in the president’s trade war.

item.title: Here Are the Key Takeaways From Kevin Warsh’s Fed Confirmation Hearing
item.description: Here are the key takeaways from Kevin Warsh’s remarks at his confirmation hearing to be the next chairman of the Federal Reserve. He testified before the US Senate Banking Committee on Tuesday.

item.title: Citadel Securities Takes On Banks With Push for Big Stock Trades
item.description: Jamie Dimon’s letter to JPMorgan Chase & Co. shareholders published this month runs to about 25,000 words. For Wall Street, two in particular stand out: “Citadel Securities.”

item.title: US Says It Boarded Oil Tanker, First During Iranian Blockade
item.description: The US government said it stopped and boarded a sanctioned oil tanker in its campaign to disrupt Iran’s shipping network, the first such intervention since the imposition of a blockade just over a week ago.

item.title: Mercuria Was Able to Get Ships Out Through Hormuz, CEO Says
item.description: Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. was reasonably well positioned before the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, and has been able to get ships out even after war erupted at the end of February, its co-founder and chief executive officer said.

item.title: Google’s Internal Politics Leave It Playing Catch-Up on AI Coding
item.description: At Google, leaders are anxious about falling behind in the race to offer AI coding tools, especially as rivals like Anthropic PBC offer more effective and popular tools to businesses, according to people familiar with the matter. The search giant is now working to unite some of its coding initiatives under one banner to speed progress and take advantage of a surge in customer interest.

item.title: Wall Street’s Crypto Ambitions Dealt a Setback by Latest Hack
item.description: The weekend hack that drained nearly $300 million from a small crypto project and triggered a $10 billion run on the largest decentralized lending platform could slow Wall Street’s growing interest in blockchain technology, according to Jefferies LLC.

item.title: Tax-the-Rich Boosts Appeal of Bonds That Give Shelter to Wealthy
item.description: Democrats around the country are pushing forward on plans to tax the rich — moves that are supportive for the tax-exempt municipal-bond market.

item.title: Xbox Cuts Game Pass Price, Will No Longer Include New ‘Call of Duty’
item.description: Less than a year after hiking the price of Game Pass, Xbox is bringing it back down.

item.title: Avala Gains 15% in April on AI, Erasing Double-Digit March Loss
item.description: Divya Nettimi’s stock-picking hedge fund Avala Global soared 15% in the first two-and-a-half weeks of April, erasing March losses after snapping up artificial intelligence bets at a discount.

item.title: Hackers Behind $300 Million Crypto Theft Now Laundering Loot
item.description: The hackers behind the largest crypto theft of the year are racing to launder their loot, even as parts of the industry scramble to block them.

item.title: Warsh Says He’ll ‘Absolutely Not’ Be President’s Sock Puppet
item.description: Kevin Warsh said he will be an independent actor as head of the Federal Reserve.

item.title: UK Proposes Law Banning Mobile Phones at Schools in England
item.description: The UK government is set to ban students from using phones at schools in England, formalizing existing guidance for teachers as it looks to cut the amount of time young people spend online and on social media.

item.title: Canadian Telecom Rogers Goes Into Tailspin as Price War Heats Up
item.description: Rogers Communications Inc. has gone from the top performer among Canada’s three big telecommunications companies to the sector’s biggest drag, as a wireless price war threatens its premium valuation.

item.title: MUFG Bank Seeks More Acquisitions After $4.3 Billion India Deal
item.description: MUFG Bank Ltd. is looking for opportunities to make further acquisitions or investments in Asia and elsewhere, according to the new head of the lending unit at Japan’s largest banking group.

item.title: HORMUZ TRACKER: Traffic Stays at a Trickle of Iran-Aligned Ships
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz remained largely devoid of commercial traffic on Tuesday as Iran continues enforcing a renewed closure of the waterway, leaving only a trickle of small vessels passing through. Most have ties to Tehran but are staying local.

item.title: Trump Urges Judge Not to Let Dimon ‘Escape’ JPMorgan Lawsuit
item.description: Donald Trump’s lawyer said JPMorgan Chase & Co. Chief Executive Officer Jamie Dimon can’t “escape liability” for allegedly blacklisting the president after Jan. 6, 2021.

item.title: Warsh Says Fed Needs New Framework for Addressing Inflation
item.description: Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s nominee to lead the US central bank, said the Federal Reserve needed a new framework for dealing with persistent inflation, without offering more specifics.

item.title: Brad Jacobs on His Big Bet on Building Insulation
item.description: The famed dealmaker on his latest acquisition.

item.title: Tiny Iran-Linked Tanker Tests US Blockade With Arabian Sea Move
item.description: A tiny tanker that’s reportedly transporting liquefied gas from Iran is testing a US blockade of the Islamic Republic’s shipping.

item.title: Odd Lots: QXO’s Brad Jacobs on His Big Insulation Bet (Podcast)
item.description: He’s done it again. On Sunday night, building supply company QXO announced that it would be acquiring TopBuild for $17 billion. TopBuild sells and installs insulation for both the residential and commercial markets. For Brad Jacobs, the CEO of QXO, this is just the latest in a lifetime of deals he’s made. In fact, he’s made over 500 deals in his life across numerous public companies that he’s founded, most of which have XO somewhere in the ticker. Brad’s companies all tend to be highly focused o
